ddls:
CREATE TABLE "proteins" (
  "protein_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each protein */,
  "pdb_code" TEXT /* PDB code of the protein */,
  "sequence" TEXT /* Sequence of the protein */,
  "resolution" REAL /* Resolution of the protein structure in Å */,
  "publication_date" TEXT /* Date when the protein structure was published */,
  "method" TEXT /* Method used to determine the protein structure (e.g., X-ray, NMR, Cryo-EM) */,
  "chain_id" TEXT /* Chain identifier of the protein in the PDB file */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("protein_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "helices" (
  "helix_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each helix */,
  "protein_id" INTEGER /* ID of the protein the helix belongs to */,
  "helix_start" INTEGER /* Start position of the helix */,
  "helix_end" INTEGER /* End position of the helix */,
  "kink_position" INTEGER /* Position of the kink in the helix */,
  "kink_angle" REAL /* Angle of the kink in the helix */,
  "helix_type" TEXT /* Type of helix (e.g., α-helix, 310-helix) */,
  "confidence_score" REAL /* Confidence score for the helix kink detection */,
  "helix_length" INTEGER /* Length of the helix */,
  "structural_class" TEXT /* Structural class of the helix (e.g., globular, fibrous) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("helix_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_helices_protein_id FOREIGN KEY ("protein_id") REFERENCES proteins ("protein_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* User's account status (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
column_names: Resolution of the protein structure in Å
Date when the protein structure was published
Method used to determine the protein structure (e.g., X-ray, NMR, Cryo-EM)
Chain identifier of the protein in the PDB file
ID of the protein the helix belongs to
Position of the kink in the helix
Angle of the kink in the helix
Confidence score for the helix kink detection
Structural class of the helix (e.g., globular, fibrous)
Role of the user (e.g., researcher, admin)
Timestamp of the user's last login
